content.problemStatement:
This is the hard version of the problem. Here, there are updates to the array, and you need to report the answer after every update.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN, as well as an integer KKK (2≤K≤N2 \le K \le N2≤K≤N).

A sequence of indices (i1,i2,…,ir)(i_1, i_2, \ldots, i_r)(i1​,i2​,…,ir​) is said to be valid if:


i1<i2<…<iri_1 \lt i_2 \lt \ldots \lt i_ri1​<i2​<…<ir​

That is, the indices are viewed in increasing order.


ij+1≠ij+1i_j + 1 \ne i_{j+1}ij​+1=ij+1​ for every 1≤j<r1 \le j \lt r1≤j<r

That is, no two chosen indices are adjacent.


i1≤K,ir≥N−K+1i_1 \le K, i_r \ge N-K+1i1​≤K,ir​≥N−K+1, and ij+1−ij≤Ki_{j+1} - i_j \le Kij+1​−ij​≤K for each 1≤j<r1 \le j \lt r1≤j<r

In simpler words, among any contiguous segment of length KKK, at least one index must be chosen.


Note that in particular, choosing a single index gives a valid sequence (as long as the chosen index is both ≤K\le K≤K and ≥N−K+1\ge N-K+1≥N−K+1).

The score of a valid sequence of indices (i1,i2,…,ir)(i_1, i_2, \ldots, i_r)(i1​,i2​,…,ir​) is defined to be

max⁡(Ai1,Ai2,…,Air)−min⁡(Ai1,Ai2,…,Air)\max(A_{i_1}, A_{i_2}, \ldots, A_{i_r}) - \min(A_{i_1}, A_{i_2}, \ldots, A_{i_r})max(Ai1​​,Ai2​​,…,Air​​)−min(Ai1​​,Ai2​​,…,Air​​)That is, the score equals the maximum among elements at the chosen indices, minus the minimum among elements at the chosen indices.

You are given the array AAA and the integer KKK.

You are also given a parameter MMM.

You must process QQQ updates to the array AAA.

Each update is of the following form:


You are given an integer XXX.

For each index iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), update AiA_iAi​ to (Ai+X) mod M(A_i + X) \bmod M(Ai​+X)modM.
After each update, compute the **maximum** possible score of a valid sequence of indices of the current array.

Note that updates to the array are permanent.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains four space-separated integers N,K,M,N, K, M,N,K,M, and QQQ — the length of the array, the separation parameter, the modulus, and the number of updates.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains QQQ space-separated integers X1,X2,…,XQX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_QX1​,X2​,…,XQ​, the update parameters.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line QQQ space-separated integers. The iii-th of them should denote the maximum possible score of a valid sequence after applying the first iii updates.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of QQQ over all test cases both won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Initially, A=[5,1,3]A = [5, 1, 3]A=[5,1,3].

The first update is with X=2X = 2X=2.
The array changes to [(5+2) mod 6,(1+2) mod 6,(3+2) mod 6]=[1,3,5][(5+2)\bmod 6, (1+2)\bmod 6, (3+2)\bmod 6] = [1, 3, 5][(5+2)mod6,(1+2)mod6,(3+2)mod6]=[1,3,5].

The optimal subsequence is (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) with max⁡(A1,A3)−min⁡(A1,A3)=5−1=4\max(A_1, A_3) - \min(A_1, A_3) = 5-1 = 4max(A1​,A3​)−min(A1​,A3​)=5−1=4.


The second update is with X=1X = 1X=1.
The array changes to [(1+1) mod 6,(3+1) mod 6,(5+1) mod 6]=[2,4,0][(1+1)\bmod 6, (3+1)\bmod 6, (5+1)\bmod 6] = [2, 4, 0][(1+1)mod6,(3+1)mod6,(5+1)mod6]=[2,4,0].

The optimal subsequence is (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) with max⁡(A1,A3)−min⁡(A1,A3)=2−0=2\max(A_1, A_3) - \min(A_1, A_3) = 2-0 = 2max(A1​,A3​)−min(A1​,A3​)=2−0=2.


The third update is with X=3X = 3X=3.
The array changes to [5,1,3][5, 1, 3][5,1,3].
The optimal subsequence is still (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) with a score of 222.
